strengths: Previous REVIEW_FAIL is resolved: DELETE /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/members/:userId route now exists (routes.ts:296-324) and is the call site for checkCanRemoveUser (routes.ts:314), satisfying the 'admin removes owner leo -> 403' deliverable.

All four pure authorization/cap helpers (checkActorCanManage, checkCanRemoveUser, checkInviteeCap, CAP_ERROR_MESSAGE, WORKSPACE_LIMIT) are exported and unit-tested; server (4) and client (9) tests pass.
Non-member access returns uniform 404 across members/manage/transfer/delete routes (routes.ts:196,224,304,410,461) per the CRITICAL sandwich constraint.
Route handlers use pool.query directly with no service factory or repository pattern, matching existing card/column route style as required by the QUALITY BAR.

Workspace delete relies on schema-level ON DELETE CASCADE from columns/cards/card_events/settings/members to workspaces (schema.sql:94,104,112-115), correctly implementing 'hard cascade all scoped data' without core module changes.

Client api.ts wrappers use path-prefixed /api/workspaces routes including getWorkspaceMembers, and client types (Workspace, WorkspaceMember, WorkspaceInvite, WorkspaceRole, WorkspaceListResponse) match the server response shape.
Transactional flows (create, accept invite, transfer-ownership) wrap multi-statement writes in BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK; existing settings tests remain green after appended workspace tests.

issues.description: addWorkspaceMember/POST members accepts role 'admin' on add (routes.ts:235-236, api.ts:138). The deliverable specifies default role 'member'; the default IS member, but allowing an explicit admin grant on add is a small scope addition not described in the deliverable. Not forbidden by spec; classify as Minor.

transfer-ownership does not guard against newOwnerId === req.user.id; self-transfer would set self to owner then immediately demote self, leaving the workspace effectively ownerless. Edge case not covered by the deliverable.
DELETE members route does not publish a membership.removed SSE event. This is deferred to the later real-time task per spec scope, not a T3 gap; noted for traceability only.

GET/POST members handlers are bare async handlers without try/catch, so a DB error yields an unhandled rejection. This matches the existing card/column route pattern in the same file (e.g., /board, /columns), so per Stage 2 'follow existing patterns' guidance it is not a T3-specific regression.
